item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just stumbled onto this article today. I've been picking out random series 3's to read, and this was expertly written. I see it had some revisions in the past, which have obviously helped shape this into a much better story. Overall this was a brilliant read, I enjoyed the portrayal of the vilagers, the anomaly and the subtle hints that led to Sarkicism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember when I read this the first time. Always been one of my favorites.

The whole sarkicism thing is really interesting and neat, some may say the whole shtick of a remote and isolated village cut off from the rest of the world and controlled by a supernatural evil is formulaic, particularly to this author, and I'd agree, but he also does it so incredibly well that I can't complain.
There's clearly an idea with these interconnecting stories, and there's an important consistency to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image #1 (Village)
Source: Wikimedia Commons
Author: Frank J. McGrath
Licence: Public Domain

Image #2 (People)
Source: Wikimedia Commons
Author: Unknown
Licence: Public Domain

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this related to the Flesh that Hates in any way? after reading through it all, it reminds me alot of the Flesh. Now that im thinking about it, is it possible that one of these Caverns has an outlet thats near the site of 610? These caverns do seem to spread for miles and miles around… And then theres the "Where the gods of Rust and Blood come to Die". The "Rust" part reminds me of the Broken God, since its all metalic and can rust, and the "Blood" Thing could be 343, the "real" god… But if this is where they come to die, then what is this "church" actually revering… This one just makes me keep thinking… Definitely and upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember the first time when i read this.

This is an excellent article, it perfectly portrays what a sarkic society looks like and and what awaits humanity under the rule of Ion. The intervention of GRU-P trying to control the town, without knowing that the most serious thing was under the mountain.

+1 dude ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A fantastic piece to read after a long, long hiatus.

Love the descriptiveness of it all. Detail is everything, to me at least. The premise ties together fantastically towards the end with the slow, agonizing crawl below the mountain.

Fabulous job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This gave me a scare, and the grubs with human heads almost made me cringe (in a disgusted way, not a OMG this is so cancer way)

+1

item.title: Re: Historical inaccuracy
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I will make that correction.

item.title: Historical inaccuracy
item.content:encoded:
While overall I'd say that the peasants you have described are very authentic (down to them eating lots of turnips, though why don't they grow any bread?), there is one passage that irks me:

Their lifestyle has been noted to be similar to that of 14th century serfdom

Not only did serfdom in Russia began in the early 16th century in earnest, with 1497 being the beginning of peasant enslavement, serfs were never common in Ural even at its heyday. It would be better to describe them as a peasant obschina, or commune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where's Myers? We need a status update.

Briggs… Oh god. Oh god. Something got him.

Nice MBTI reference there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to refrain from voting, then I read the last log.
I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did some digging through my draft history and found the original addendum for this. A bit of a spoiler, in that it revealed/explained too much and people suggested I cut this part. Still, I liked it and thought I would post it here.

+ Spoilers
- ACCESS GRANTED

Addendum:

SCP-2122 was reclaimed with minimal causalities. SCP-2122-2 was again found to cover the village like roots and vines. A new instance of SCP-2122-1 was found working in the field. Since classified as SCP-2122-1-37, the instance strongly resembles Dr. Felsenstein and is dressed in a shredded Foundation issued Hazmat suit missing its mask portion. SCP-2122-1-37 has ignored all efforts at communication. A recovered hair sample was analyzed and found to be a genetic match for Dr. Felsenstein. Despite being biologically alive, it was decided to list Dr. Felsenstein as deceased. He has since been posthumously awarded the Foundation Star for his self-sacrifice.

A small structure far from the village center was discovered inhabited by an instance of SCP-2122-1, now classified as SCP-2122-1-59. Access Interview 04 for further details.

- ACCESS GRANTED
Interviewed: SCP-2122-1-59, "Bogumil". Has the appearance of a male, approximately three to five years of age. Eye sockets are empty and heavily infected. Although unable to leave SCP-2122 like other SCP-2122 instances, it has chosen to remain in a shack at the far end of the village and is not recorded as ever having entered the church.

Interviewer: Dr. Judith Low

Foreword: The only SCP-2122-1 instance willing to speak to the Foundation after the forced evacuation of personnel and the subsequent re-containment of SCP-2122.

<Begin Log>

Dr. Judith Low: Hello. I don't believe we've met yet. Do you mind if we speak?

SCP-2122-1-59: Hello. I've not spoken with another for so long. You are behind the recent turbulence, are you not? I apologize on behalf my former flock. Our village was not always like this.

Dr. Judith Low: Why have you removed yourself from the others?

SCP-2122-1-59: A difference of faith. Would it be correct to assume you desire a more complete answer?

Dr. Judith Low: If you are willing. I must say, you seem quite educated. Your vocabulary and dialect greatly differ from the others.

SCP-2122-1-59: I was not born here and learned much as part of the Church. Ignore my current appearance. I was once a priest of the Eastern Orthodoxy. I came here to spread the Christian faith after the fall of the Khanate of Sibir. I was a young man when I arrived. Life was difficult but I kept hope alive.

For a time.

I was an old man when the pagans came to our humble village. It was a time of great suffering. We called it the Cruelest Year, when famine and plague ravaged the region. Despite our prayers, we were inescapably doomed.

And thus, the newcomers seemed a godsend to the people. All of whom ignored my warnings.

Dr. Judith Low: Tell me of these outsiders.

SCP-2122-1-59: Pagans. Believers in a very old and blasphemous faith. Older than the Khanate. The heathen shamans worthy of Glorification compared to these villains. They arrived by the hundreds - survivors of neighboring villages shepherded by their prophets. Where the prophets came from, I cannot say. One had a Smolensk tongue13, the rest were clearly foreign.

The heathen priests were three in number. They wielded the dark arts and called it divine. The people befouled themselves to appease the appetites of their wicked masters. Such a vile faith.

Dr. Judith Low: Do you speak of the Church of the Red Harvest?

SCP-2122-1-59: Yes. An appropriate name for reasons beyond the overt; reasons I dare not repeat.

Dr. Judith Low: Who were these prophets?

SCP-2122-1-59: I remember them vividly. Karcist Varis14 was the only Russian among them.

Dr. Judith Low: I apologize for interrupting but did you say Karcist?

SCP-2122-1-59: Aye. That is what they called themselves. Sorcerers that feigned holiness. They spoke of their master and his eventual return. They spoke as if it was the Second Coming. The Grand Karcist is no messiah - he is Koschei the Deathless15.

Dr. Judith Low: What of the others?

SCP-2122-1-59: Karcist Varis and Karcist Szivak did not remain long. It was Karcist Alka who stayed and claimed this land as her domain.

Dr. Judith Low: Tell me of Karcist Alka.

SCP-2122-1-59: Karcist Alka - an angelic visage with a most fiendish heart.

Dr. Judith Low: What happened to Karcist Alka?

SCP-2122-1-59: She remains. You will find pieces of her throughout the village. She is strong. She is eternal. All while we lament in our circle of suffering.

I've refused to enter that church for hundreds of years. It is no longer a true church. Just a gatehouse.

Dr. Judith Low: What do you mean by gatehouse?

SCP-2122-1-59: I built my church atop the ruins of a pagan temple. But that temple, like my church, merely covered something much older. There is a demon shrine under the mountains. That is where the others are. Waiting.

Dr. Judith Low: Waiting for what?

SCP-2122-1-59: For the return of paradise – the arrival of Adytum. May your souls be prepared.

Dr. Judith Low: I thank you for this conversation. This has been most illuminating.

SCP-2122-1-59: Aye. Go. Less something worse than death claims you.

Dr. Judith Low: Sorry but one more question, if you do not mind? You were a priest. Did you return to your old faith after removing yourself from the community?

SCP-2122-1-59: Partially. Yes. I believe in one God, the Father Almighty, Maker of heaven and earth, and of all things visible and invisible. But to allow our anguish, I now know that he is a God of Suffering and Tears.

And to him I will never bow again.

<End Log>

Closing Statement: I may now comfortably state that the denizens of SCP-2122 represent practitioners of Proto-Sarkicism16. I request the transfer of SCP-2122 to the direct control of Project: Sitra Achra.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that the "Village with creepy body horror people" is a bit of a cliche, but this is executed very well. The 6-year-old talking about Ion, the mutated GRU-P operative, it all establishes a beautiful atmosphere of horror. One of the things that I like the most about this SCP is that you didn't fall for the "MTF goes into creepy place and creepy stuff happpens AND THEY ALL DIE!" cliche, instead showing what comes after the dying. And it's glorious.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is well-trod horror territory, but you gotta admire that execution!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am really liking all of the Sarkic articles, and this is no exception. I think the log is written realistically, and is really disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a little uninterested as I started reading and felt a no vote was coming. As I got deeper into the rabbit hole, the +1 was a no-brainer. Great entry! The initial description of the town gave me a Resident Evil 4 vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is terrifying. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Will be my last for the time being.

Say it ain't so! Your Sarkic stuff is amazing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This pretty much sums it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Metaphysician does it again. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! That means a lot. Yes - I can't quite recall where that village was but a different system if I recall; sacrificing their children to remain young - or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I definitely remember reading anothe reincarnation village in Russia, in Series II, but that didn't stop me from enjoying this immensely. It's a great, creepy premise and the horror really works. It's a descent into madness and terror. Being home alone for five days, I might have a tough time sleeping tonight. Enthusiastic upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I did try to do research on it and ultimately, I was aiming for maximum allowance of such canisters but didn't consider it in terms of the Hazmat suit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Major nitpick: a typical SCBA tank holds about 15 minutes of air, 20 if you stretch it (the manuals on those things say they're thirty-minute tanks, but the fucker who writes the manual has never been inside a burning building). In addition to the weight of a Type A HAZMAT suit (probably 40lbs (~18 kilos)), scientific and/or Action Science!!! equipment (probably another 40lbs/18kilos), a thirty-minute tank might weigh 20 lbs/9 kilos.

This means that a Type A HAZMAT suit with three hours of air would weigh approximately one US Standard fuckton (unfortunately, I don't know the conversion rate on fucktons, so you folks across the pond will have to Google it). In other words, if a normal Type A HAZMAT suit is a spacesuit, these things would be space stations, or possibly the Jaegers from Pacific Rim (which would be awesome, but not realistic or cosmic horror-themed).

My suggestion? Downgrade the suits to Type B; those have gas masks or powered air filters, weigh less, and would probably keep the explorers safe from the super-rabies these folks carry.

Otherwise, I'm digging it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2133, along with -2075, -2095, 2191, -2406 (through references and implications), 2480, and 2510 (again, through references and implications) are tied together - a sort of mythos I felt the urge to build up. Been pacing myself, taking it slow - I have one that I am really excited about. Will be my last for the time being.

Adytum - a very old city and its unnumbered, terrible crimes.

I am actually working on a collection of lore for this - written from the Foundation's point of view. A sort of "this is what we know so far". Feel free to look through the draft, tab Sarkic Hub: On Sarkicism http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/metaphysician less designed to be entertaining and more a specific source material that could be used.

The villagers in this story don't know much - a relic of the old days. Just waiting to be called on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to be honest, the actual item itself, the village and its inhabitants, is not terribly interesting. I mean, there's a whole crapton of villages out of three hundred years ago on the site. Two things made me upvote, though.

First, I think I've finally figured out the whole "Adytum" thing. I mean, noticed that it keeps getting mentioned, and I went and looked it up and now maybe it'll stick in my head.

Second? Those exploration logs. Well, and the interviews. But the exploration logs especially felt like the first time I read through the Flesh. And without just being a rehash of it, too. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edit: Fixed! Yay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new moon actually has no real effect - it is a tradition that has meaning to them but a meaning they have not divulged. Yeah, I'm always happy with people notice a lot of the little things:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let me start by saying +1
I think my favorite thing about this is all the little details; the creepy dialogue, the eldritch church, the hysterical doctor, the very minute detail of having 'access granted' when you look at the docs, and especially the "Maz Hatters"
Plus it's always fun when the foundation thinks something's going to be easy and it turns out to be a little less simple.

I think the only problems I have are a few cliches, which are bound to come up in any SCP article. The "new moon" thing specifically stuck me as strange? Is it significant in some way to the religion? If not, changing it to "An infant will subsequently be found some amount of unspecified days later. The number of days has not been observed to exceed 28." might suit you. Not a huge deal though, just a personal nit-pick.

Also nice job putting the information that they take their turnips and "gangrenous limbs and tumorous growths" to the church and that they have a high-protein diest all within a few sentences of each other. Nice tip-off without necessarily spelling it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see that this article now sees the light of day and that you have cleaned it up sufficiently. Upvote is obvious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I'm bad with numbers and measurements ;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should be "mass" not "weight" (or it should be in Newtons not kilogram but that would be weird).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird. It was supposed to be. I'll fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brassica rapa should be italicized.

Otherwise you continue to be my favorite author here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup. Forgot to change that. Was a copy and paste related mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interview 01: 09/14/1992
Interview 02: 10/16/1991

Mix-up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "This is the land where gods of rust and blood come to die." Considering the appearance of the speaker, that hit the perfect note of chilling for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was posted months and months ago but I took it down - it was in its 30s but one person said it was boring so I've been tinkering with it. Funny, its old number was 2122.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the horror film The Descent. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cheerful upvote, lots here to like, lots to be intrigued by, and very nicely written.
I hope it's okay, your autopsy report measured weight in 'km' - I changed it to 'kg'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Well done, Metaphysician. :) +1.
